text:
Market today: Mood is cautiously optimistic for AI/chips. The key headline is Mag 7 losing $2.3T on AI spending jitters, but investors are still backing chipmakers specifically - that's the tug-of-war driving your book right now. One headline hints the AI trade could re-accelerate in H2, which is the real potential catalyst for names like CRWD, SMH, SOXL.

Your holdings: SOXL (-7.06%) is your biggest concern - it's a 3x leveraged semiconductor ETF that decays and is sitting uncomfortably close to your $200 stop; on margin, a bad day here hits hard. SPCX (+0.53%) is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no price history - margin amplifies any sudden drop. SMH (-0.69%) is mild but worth watching given the AI spending jitter headlines.

Watch: 1) Whether chipmakers hold their bid as the Mag 7 AI spending narrative plays out. 2) SOXL's $200 stop - do not let that level break without acting.

Bottom line: Protect the leveraged and new positions first; let the AI re-acceleration thesis prove itself before adding risk.
plays: SOXL: Your stop is $200 and you're down 7% on a 3x leveraged name on margin - tighten mentally or set a hard alert. One bad chip headline closes that gap fast.
SPCX: Brand-new IPO, nearly zero track record, margin account. Keep size small and your $150 stop firm - no reason to add here yet.
No new buys today - the AI spending jitter overhang on Mag 7 is unresolved; let the dust settle before layering on more chip exposure.
